channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Pentagon cuts US brigade presence in Europe, delays Poland deployment
item.description: The US is reducing its brigade presence in Europe and delaying a planned deployment to Poland, marking a significant shift in US military posture on the continent amid ongoing questions about American commitment to NATO allies.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US Senate advances resolution to limit Trump's Iran war powers
item.description: The US Senate advanced a war powers resolution that would require congressional authorization for continued US involvement in the Iran conflict, with a Republican senator who lost his primary becoming a key defector in a surprise blow to the administration.

item.title: ⚖️🇮🇱 ICC prosecutor seeks arrest warrant for Israeli Finance Minister Smotrich
item.description: Israel's far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich announced that the ICC prosecutor is seeking a confidential arrest warrant against him, and he vowed to retaliate by waging "war" on the Palestinian Authority, ordering the eviction of a West Bank Palestinian village.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran rejects US school strike claim as baseless fabrication
item.description: Tehran dismissed US accusations of targeting a school as a "baseless fabrication," saying targeting an educational institution during school hours would constitute a grave violation of international humanitarian law.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳💻 US House foreign affairs chair warns AI sales to China could make America a loser
item.description: House Foreign Affairs Chair Brian Mast warned that American firms must not let US AI technology reach the "wrong places," stressing the strategic importance of maintaining technological advantages over China.

item.title: 🕊️ Iran Makes New Peace Proposal as Trump Postpones Military Strikes
item.description: Iran submitted a new proposal through Pakistani mediators offering nuclear program suspension and phased Hormuz reopening. Trump delayed planned attacks after Gulf states intervened, though both sides remain deadlocked on key demands.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Cancels Planned Iran Attack After Gulf States Request Delay
item.description: President Trump postponed military strikes on Iran after Gulf nations requested time for negotiations. Iran submitted a revised 14-point proposal via Pakistani mediators, but key disputes over nuclear enrichment and the Strait of Hormuz remain unresolved.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Shifts to Strategic Neutralization Strategy Against Russia
item.description: Ukraine is pursuing asymmetric warfare to degrade Russia economy and military infrastructure rather than costly territorial offensives. Deep strikes on Russian oil refineries and military facilities are disrupting Moscow war effort and challenging Russia spring offensive.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇸🇴 Somaliland and Israel Announce Mutual Embassies in Historic Move
item.description: Somaliland will open an embassy in Jerusalem while Israel establishes diplomatic presence in Hargeisa. The move follows Israel official recognition of the breakaway Somali regions independence.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Charges 7 Chinese Executives in Global Container Cartel Crackdown
item.description: The US Justice Department charged executives from companies that manufacture 95% of the world's shipping containers for price-fixing. The move targets China's dominance over global logistics infrastructure.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Iran Warns of New Fronts as US Threatens Large-Scale Assault
item.description: Tehran threatened to open new conflict fronts after Washington warned of potential large-scale military operations. Trump said he was "an hour away" from striking Iran before Gulf allies requested delay.

item.title: 🇺🇦💔 UN: Over 15,800 Killed in Russia's War on Ukraine
item.description: The United Nations confirmed nearly 800 children are among the more than 15,800 people killed since Russia's full-scale invasion began in February 2022.

item.title: 🛢️💰 US Crude Inventories Fall 9.1 Million Barrels
item.description: API reported crude inventories fell by 9.1 million barrels, though stocks remain higher than last year. Oil prices stay above $100 as Iran conflict continues.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO Jet Shoots Down Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia
item.description: A Romanian F-16 deployed with NATO intercepted and downed a Ukrainian drone over southern Estonia. Ukraine apologized for the incident, while Russia threatened retaliation if Baltic countries allow their territory to be used for attacks.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Plans to Shrink NATO Crisis Forces
item.description: The Trump administration will tell NATO allies it plans to reduce the pool of US military capabilities available for alliance defense during crises. The move reflects Trumps expectation that European nations take primary responsibility for continental security.

item.title: 🏦 Standard Chartered to Cut Over 7,000 Jobs in AI Push
item.description: Standard Chartered announced it will eliminate more than 7,000 jobs over four years, replacing lower-value human capital with AI technology. The cuts target 15% of corporate function roles as the bank seeks to improve profitability and efficiency.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Rubio Heads to NATO Meeting, First Visit to India
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet NATO foreign ministers in Sweden on Friday to discuss defense burden-sharing, then make his first visit to India. The trip follows US troop withdrawal announcements and strained ties with India over trade and diplomatic issues.

item.title: 🇱🇧 At Least 22 Killed in Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Lebanon Health Ministry reports 22 deaths in 24 hours from Israeli strikes, bringing the total death toll since March 2 to 3,042. Israel issued forced displacement orders for 12 towns in southern Lebanon.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Minister Smotrich Claims ICC Prosecutor Seeking Secret Arrest Warrant
item.description: Far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said the ICC prosecutor requested a secret arrest warrant against him, calling it "a declaration of war." He retaliated by ordering the demolition of the Palestinian Bedouin village of Khan al-Ahmar.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia sidelines Lavrov from Ukraine peace talks
item.description: Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has been cut out of Moscow's Ukraine negotiations, with officials saying he has 'no say' in peace talks. Other Kremlin figures like Yuri Ushakov now drive Russia's outreach to Washington.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK quietly issues waivers for Russian oil imports
item.description: The UK issued licenses allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel made from Russian oil if processed in third countries, plus a separate license for maritime transport of Russian LNG. The waivers come amid rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese carrier Liaoning begins western Pacific drills
item.description: China's Liaoning aircraft carrier group launched live-fire exercises in the western Pacific, months after a stand-off with Japanese fighter jets during December training. The drills come amid tense Beijing-Tokyo relations.

item.title: ⚔️ MQ-25A Stingray cleared for deployment
item.description: The US Navy's first carrier-based unmanned aircraft reached milestone C, moving into production and deployment. The Boeing Stingray will relieve F/A-18 Super Hornets from aerial refueling duties, extending carrier strike group reach.

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo a Global Health Emergency
item.description: A rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has killed at least 131 people in eastern Congo, prompting the WHO to declare a public health emergency of international concern as cases spread to Uganda.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Considers Secret Military Alliance to Replace NATO Dependence
item.description: The Economist reports European officials are quietly developing contingency plans for collective defense without US involvement, amid growing concerns about American commitment to the alliance.
